status.next_allowed_action: Reduce static-renderer.ts to composition and compatibility only for task 5.3.

decision_log.reason: The work spans graph-engine architecture, workbench integration, offline HTML, browser regression scripts, SpatialIndex, community wash behavior, and old renderer cleanup.

decision_log.decision: Create a child implementation branch only when execution starts
decision_log.reason: The current step is document planning. Code implementation needs a dedicated branch from the current architecture branch so the previous work remains reviewable.
decision_log.source: user direction and AGENTS.md branch rule

decision_log.decision: Keep DOM and SVG for this architecture pass
decision_log.reason: The known failures come from interaction ownership and browser default behavior, not proven rendering throughput limits.

decision_log.decision: Reuse existing Node tests and browser regression scripts by default
decision_log.reason: The repo already has graph-engine tests and Playwright-driven regression scripts through npx; adding a new dependency is not required for the plan.

decision_log.decision: SpatialIndex must become the actual graph hit-testing source
decision_log.reason: The user explicitly wanted this recorded so it is not forgotten, and the design rejects interface-only completion.

decision_log.decision: Keep static-renderer.ts only as a thin compatibility shell if it remains
decision_log.reason: The desired cleanup is deleting old renderer responsibilities, not forcing a risky filename deletion when a narrow shell preserves public API compatibility.
decision_log.source: design review and implementation surface inspection

decision_log.decision: Graph-owned wheel, trackpad wheel, and pinch-like ctrl/meta wheel must zoom the graph, not the browser page
decision_log.reason: The graph must own interaction on nodes, edges, community washes, and blank canvas. Text editing surfaces remain blockers.

decision_log.decision: Use d3-quadtree as the SpatialIndex node-search core
decision_log.reason: A mature D3 spatial-search primitive is already present through d3-force's dependency tree and is a better foundation than hand-rolling a uniform grid.

decision_log.decision: Make graph world bounds layout-driven instead of treating 1000x680 as a hard interaction cage
decision_log.reason: Pinned and dragged outlier nodes must not be silently clamped back by the old render model or viewport world size.

decision_log.decision: Execute the interaction vertical chain before extracting GraphFacade
decision_log.reason: User chose plan-eng-review D3 option A so the first real ownership transfer is Browser event -> GraphGestures -> SpatialIndex -> GraphViewport/GraphState -> GraphRenderer, avoiding facade-only progress.

phases.goal: Open the implementation branch and record the full interaction risk audit before changing behavior.

acceptance.check: SpatialIndex covers node, edge, community wash, blank, overlap priority, and out-of-world cases.
evidence: packages/graph-engine/src/layout/spatial-index.ts implements GraphSpatialIndex using d3-quadtree for node candidates and a unified hitTest API for node, edge, community wash, and graph blank targets.

packages/graph-engine/test/spatial-index.test.ts covers node bounds, edge tolerance, community ellipse hits, blank fallback, node-over-edge/community priority, edge-over-community priority, nodes outside the old 1000x680 world, and drag/pin rebuild behavior.
GraphSpatialIndex snapshots node coordinates at construction so moving nodes requires an explicit rebuild instead of mutating quadtree coordinates in place.

acceptance.check: Graph-owned target and blocker contracts are covered without relying on DOM stacking order.
evidence: GRAPH_OWNED_TARGET_KINDS and GRAPH_GESTURE_BLOCKER_TARGET_KINDS define graph-owned vs blocker targets.
isGraphOwnedGestureTarget, isGraphGestureBlockerTarget, and graphGestureTargetOwnership expose the ownership contract.
gestures.test.ts covers the stable graph-owned/blocker contract without DOM stacking order.
interaction-contract.test.ts proves SpatialIndex graph-object hits can drive GraphGestures pointer/wheel decisions even when a fake DOM target would classify the same point as a community wash.

phases.goal: Make GraphGestures the sole raw input and browser-default policy owner.

acceptance.check: Graph-owned wheel, trackpad-like wheel, pointer, and graph-focused keyboard behavior is routed through GraphGestures.
evidence: GraphGestureController owns root wheel, pointerdown, pointermove, pointerup, pointercancel, lostpointercapture, and double-click bindings.
gestures.test.ts covers graph-owned wheel/pointer routing, blocker boundaries, pointercancel, lostpointercapture, click-vs-drag threshold, and graph-owned shortcut wheel zoom.
keyboard.test.ts covers graph-focused shortcut and Escape ownership, while blocking text editing and outside-graph focus.

tests/graph-default-behavior.regression-1.sh covers graph-owned shortcut wheel, native selection prevention, fast drag release, cancellation cleanup, blocker boundaries, and keyboard ownership in a real browser.

acceptance.check: Graph-owned surfaces do not trigger browser page zoom, native text selection, or native drag selection.
evidence: GraphGestures routes graph-owned ctrl/meta wheel over nodes, edges, community washes, and blank canvas to graph zoom while blockers remain blocked.
GraphGestureController prevents browser defaults for graph-owned pointerdown/move/up/cancel sequences while leaving search and other blockers alone.
static-renderer root CSS sets overscroll-behavior: contain, touch-action: none, and user-select: none; search and reader text surfaces restore normal text behavior.
tests/graph-default-behavior.regression-1.sh records unchanged devicePixelRatio, visualViewport.scale, documentElement.clientWidth, and documentElement.clientHeight while graph transform changes.

acceptance.check: Fast node drag release persists the final released position.
evidence: commitGraphNodeDrag accepts a finalWorldPoint and applies it to the simulation before ending and pinning the drag.
static-renderer passes the pointerup-derived node drag target into commitGraphNodeDrag, so a missing final pointermove cannot commit an old position.
node-drag-lifecycle.test.ts covers committing a fast release after the last move point differs from the final pointer target.
tests/graph-default-behavior.regression-1.sh records a browser fast-release drag where the node pins at the release point, not the intermediate move point.

acceptance.check: Workbench and offline browser regressions exit 0 with page zoom metrics recorded.
evidence: bash tests/graph-default-behavior.regression-1.sh -> PASS: graph default behavior regression; evidence includes unchanged devicePixelRatio, visualViewport.scale, clientWidth, and clientHeight while graph transform changes.

bash tests/graph-workbench-interactions.regression-1.sh -> PASS: graph workbench interaction regression; evidence includes graph-owned wheel over blank, node, community wash, and edge plus blocked wheel targets for search, toolbar, legend, minimap, and drawer.

phases.goal: Close coordinate and runtime state loops through GraphViewport and GraphState.

acceptance.check: No old renderer-local coordinate formulas drive graph behavior.
evidence: static-renderer.ts now uses geometry helpers for viewport fallback size, motion-frame world deltas, and isolated diff side anchors instead of direct renderer-local world-size formulas.
model.ts uses geometry helpers for CSS percent projection and minimap projection, and live positions are no longer clamped to the old 1000x680 world.
geometry.test.ts covers CSS percent projection outside the default world, world-point delta projection, and side-exit anchors.
render-model.test.ts covers preserving live drag positions beyond the old default world.
rg -n "rootRect\.width \*|rootRect\.height \*|eventToGraphPoint|clientX.*WORLD|clientY.*WORLD" packages/graph-engine/src/render/static-renderer.ts -> no matches

acceptance.check: Hover, selection, focus, active drag, pins, and viewport have one GraphState-owned path.
evidence: GraphRuntimeState snapshot now includes selectionSurface plus node-drag startWorldPoint and wasPinned, so reader selection, selection-panel selection, drag cancellation, and fast release use one state owner.

static-renderer.ts removed renderer-local pins, selectedNodeId, selection, manualNodeIds, focus, viewport, pending drag, and hover preview state as behavior sources; render, hover, selection, focus, drag, pins, and viewport now read/write GraphRuntimeState snapshots.
state.test.ts covers selectionSurface, drag startWorldPoint, wasPinned, cloned snapshots, and clearInteraction preserving viewport, positions, and pins.
graph-workbench-interactions regression covers zoom over graph targets, blocked UI targets, hover after zoom/drawer resize, community drag, pan/reset, and pinned drag behavior.
graph-offline-phase-6 regression covers offline zoom, hover after zoom, drag pointer distance, reader drawer, and pin persistence.
graph-default-behavior regression covers browser zoom prevention, blank drag selection prevention, pointercancel/lostpointercapture, fast release drag, and keyboard ownership.

acceptance.check: Layout bounds, minimap, fit, SpatialIndex, and hover anchors support nodes outside the old 1000x680 world.
evidence: RenderableGraph now exposes worldBounds derived from rendered node positions, including live positions and pinned positions outside the old default world.
geometry.ts projection helpers, minimap helpers, viewport helpers, overlay anchors, and drag screen/world conversion all accept GraphWorldBounds while preserving the default 1000x680 fallback.
static-renderer.ts passes graph.worldBounds through fit, pan, wheel zoom, drawer resize, minimap viewport, hover anchors, node drag, SVG viewBox, and motion-frame base-position updates.
render-model.test.ts covers live dragged positions and pinned positions outside the old default world expanding render bounds instead of clamping.
geometry.test.ts and viewport.test.ts cover expanded bounds round trips, CSS projection, minimap projection, fit/center, and viewport minimap rectangles.
graph-workbench-interactions, graph-offline-phase-6, and graph-default-behavior regressions all exit 0 after dynamic world bounds.

acceptance.check: Workbench and offline browser regressions exit 0, including old pin compatibility.
evidence: PinCoordinateSpace now distinguishes explicit world pins from old percent pins, so new pins with small or negative coordinates are not misread as percentages.
GraphLayoutFile accepts version 1 and 2; workbench and offline writers now emit version 2 while old version 1 pin maps remain readable.
render-model.test.ts covers explicit world pins whose coordinates look like old percentages plus old unmarked percent pins.
sim.test.ts covers PinState preserving old percent pins and new explicit world pins through pinsToPositions.
workbench/server/src/graph.test.ts covers reading old layout files, preserving explicit world and legacy-percent coordinateSpace markers, and filtering invalid coordinateSpace values.
graph-offline-phase-6 browser regression verifies dragged offline pins persist to localStorage with coordinateSpace: world and survive reload.
